short: Splitting a long prompt's prefill into chunks so it doesn't block other requests.
definition: Chunked prefill breaks the prefill phase of a long prompt into smaller token chunks that can be interleaved with the decode steps of other in-flight requests. Without it, one very long prompt can monopolize the GPU and spike time-to-first-token for everyone else. It is a key knob for keeping latency low in mixed short/long workloads.
